title: 24. Story Visuals
description: Story-mode Visual Reply settings for continuity, image count, visual safety, and master prompt guidance.

steps.title: Open Story Visuals
steps.body: Story Visuals lives under Host settings and controls story-mode image behavior. It does not replace the main Visual Reply runtime provider; it shapes how story prompts are built.

steps.title: Toggle Story Visual Mode
steps.body: The mode button switches story visual behavior on or off. Keep it off for text-only story tests, then enable it once the story and voice routing are stable.

steps.title: Limit Image Count
steps.body: Max images limits how many story images can be generated. Use a small value for tests before running a long story session.

steps.title: Set Continuity Strength
steps.body: Continuity helps recurring characters, places, props, and style stay consistent. Higher values hold the look tighter; lower values allow more variety.

steps.title: Use Safety and Text Guards
steps.body: Safe mode and no speech bubbles keep story images cleaner for general use. They are prompt guards, not provider-level policy controls.

steps.title: Write a Master Visual Prompt
steps.body: The master prompt is the stable visual style anchor for a story. Use it for recurring art direction, camera style, character consistency, and visual mood.
